steps.title: Trace the passage's structure
steps.content:
The passage opens by noting pteropods are thought to be vulnerable to ocean acidification. It then presents Peck and colleagues' findings: (1) the periostracum prevents shell dissolution, and (2) pteropods can rebuild the inner shell wall even when the periostracum is breached. These findings challenge the assumed vulnerability — suggesting the concern may be overstated.
  • Choice A — To call for additional research on biological mechanisms that improve pteropod survival rates: The passage presents findings; it does not call for more research. ✗
  • Choice B — To present findings that suggest that a concern about the effects of ocean acidification on pteropod shells may be unwarranted: Matches exactly: Peck et al.'s findings show pteropods have defenses against shell dissolution, suggesting the worry may be unwarranted. ✓
  • Choice C — To address some of the ways ocean acidification has altered pteropod behavior over time: The passage is about shell physiology and resilience, not behavioral changes over time. ✗
  • Choice D — To discuss a conclusion drawn in a study of calcium carbonate's role in protecting the periostracum of pteropods: Inverts the relationship — the periostracum protects against calcium carbonate dissolution, not the other way around. ✗
That matches Choice B — To present findings that suggest that a concern about the effects of ocean acidification on pteropod shells may be unwarranted.

steps.title: Confirm the main purpose
steps.content:
The passage's arc is: assumed vulnerability $\Rightarrow$ findings that challenge that assumption. Choice B — To present findings that suggest that a concern about the effects of ocean acidification on pteropod shells may be unwarranted captures this as "findings that suggest a concern … may be unwarranted." No other choice captures both the concern and the findings that undercut it.

That matches Choice B — To present findings that suggest that a concern about the effects of ocean acidification on pteropod shells may be unwarranted.
